items.title: RCMP say damage to police vehicles in First Nations protest includes 'urine-soaked interior surfaces'
Trump administration seeks $152M to reopen Alcatraz as 'state-of-the-art secure prison'
items.summary_auto: The White House released a budget on Friday, in which the Trump administration requested funds for the ⁠Federal Bureau ⁠of Prisons to cover the ‌first-year costs of rebuilding Alcatraz into "a state-of-the-art secure prison facility."

items.title: Is male friendship in trouble? Actor Andrew McCarthy went on a road trip to find out
items.summary_auto: After the author and "Brat Pack" actor's wife and son confronted him with the fact he didn't have friends anymore, McCarthy crisscrossed the country to visit old friends and ask strangers whether friendships between men are in trouble.

items.title: Israel strikes Beirut as U.S. warns Iran may target universities
items.summary_auto: Israel pounded Beirut with airstrikes on Friday that the military said ‌had targeted what it calls militant "infrastructure" in Lebanon's capital, as the U.S. warned that Iran may target universities in the country as war in the Middle East rages.

items.title: 2 deaths after giving plasma in Winnipeg not linked to donation process: Health Canada
items.summary_auto: The federal health regulator said Friday its assessment found no evidence of a machine malfunction and the equipment performed as expected, wrote Health Canada spokesperson Mark Johnson in an emailed statement.

items.title: Toronto Tempo select guard Julie Allemand as 1st-ever player in WNBA expansion draft
items.summary_auto: The Toronto Tempo selected Julie Allemand with their first pick of the WNBA expansion draft on Friday.

items.title: Champagne leaves China with pork tariffs still in place, but touts relationship-building
items.summary_auto: Although Chinese tariffs on Canadian pork products remain in place after a visit to China by Finance Minister François-Philippe Champagne, he says the important task is relationship-building — and it's not realistic to expect immediate solutions.

items.title: New mineral discovered inside deep-Earth diamond now named after Alberta geochemist
items.summary_auto: From the coalfields of northern England to the Arctic snows and the steaming jungles of Brazil, diamond hunter and scholar Graham Pearson has carved a name for himself that now lives on in rock.

items.title: Hyundai recalls some Canadian vehicles due to seatbelt issue
items.summary_auto: In some cars, a wiring issue with the seatbelt may cause the seatbelt indicator to say the belt in the third-row driver's side seat is buckled, even when it's not. Hyundai says it will notify affected drivers in writing, though drivers can also check if they are impacted by the recall online.

items.title: Dangerous bacterial infection hits highest level seen in Canada in more than a decade
Carney leans on private money, alternative approaches in nature strategy. Will it work?
items.summary_auto: The new approaches could provide an important boost to conservation in Canada and reduce the burden on Ottawa to do the work alone, but experts say if it’s not done correctly, these other-conserved areas may end up existing on paper but not providing the level of protection necessary for ecosystems to thrive.

items.title: What you should know ahead of curling’s Rock League debut season
items.summary_auto: Thought curling season was over? Well, think again. Because even after an electric ride through the Grand Slams, Olympic trials, Olympics, Scotties, Brier and world championships, there is one event still to come. Rock League — a new professional curling outfit — will make its debut Monday at Toronto’s Mattamy Athletic Centre.

items.title: Can AI apps replace teachers? Experts urge caution over school model
items.summary_auto: Imagine school being two hours of core academics learned daily via an app, without teachers, and double that time spent on hands-on experiences. The unconventional model from a U.S. private school is sparking chatter and may benefit some, but Canadian experts say the approach blending GenAI with pre-existing methods warrants serious consideration about student privacy, well-being and what kids are actually learning.

items.title: Liberal bill would force companies to simplify data access for police. No one knows the cost
items.summary_auto: The Liberal government's second attempt at giving police and spies easier access to Canadians' information includes what's anticipated to be costly demands on a range of private businesses to to change how they manage their data.

items.title: Ontario's move to give transportation minister power to set fares has advocates concerned
items.summary_auto: The Ontario government is looking to give itself the power to set transit fares across the province, a move some in Toronto are celebrating as a step towards a more integrated transit system in the GTA — while others are apprehensive about what the move could mean for the TTC.

items.title: The energy crisis is getting worse. How protected is Canada?
items.summary_auto: The pain at the pumps and the expected rise in inflation is a financial challenge for many as the cost of living continues to climb, but overall, the country is not nearly as exposed to the impacts of the energy crisis as many other countries.
